domain: Archerfish refraction-corrected water-jet targeting — fish learns an internal model of light refraction at air-water boundary via motor adaptation over trials; aftereffect proves model is internal not reactive.
specific_mechanism: Online iterative refinement of refraction-correction parameter; aftereffect on perturbation removal demonstrates persistent internal correction model.
llm_application: ARCHER-VERIFY: external evaluation-diagnostic head that maintains an online per-prompt-class refraction-correction lookup mapping observed distorted output distribution (post-temperature, post-RLHF, post-grammar-constrained) → expected un-distorted training distribution. Detects systematic model drift by measuring aftereffect-style residual (expected vs. observed) over a sliding window of prompts. Distinguishes from inference-time correction: ARCHER-VERIFY does NOT modify model outputs — only flags drift.
novelty_claim: Grammar-Aligned Decoding (ASAp) corrects distribution at decode-time. Calibration (Platt scaling, temperature scaling) adjusts global confidence. Drift-detection literature (e.g. R322 perturbation-probes) uses paired-prompt diagnostics. ARCHER-VERIFY differs by using a MOTOR-ADAPTATION-STYLE online aftereffect signal as the drift detector — flags model behavior changes that persist across prompt classes, like the archerfish's persistent aftereffect.
